channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to read any legacy codebase. The archaeology playbook.
item.description: How to read any legacy codebase. The archaeology playbook. Somewhere on a hard drive sits a folder of low resolution scans of Russian typewritten pages from the 1950s. The pages describe PP-BESM,...

item.title: Personal and Corporate Security in an Agentic World
item.description: Personal and Corporate Security in an Agentic World AI can clone your child's voice in three seconds and your company's deployment pipeline in three minutes. The security model for neither exists...

item.title: Dev.to: We had AI pitching our customers' aunts. Here's the three-axis classification fix.
item.description: The bug report A customer wrote in: Why is the tool drafting a SaaS sales pitch to my aunt? I didn't know whether to laugh or hide. We'd just shipped warm-market draft-generation in our Chrome...

item.title: Backtesting an ICT strategy at 184 speed: timezone-cache + bisect lookup
item.description: I have been running an ICT-based reversal strategy live on US500 for a few months. The strategy itself is fine, but the bottleneck was nowhere near the strategy logic. It was in the backtest...

item.title: Why Your $200 AI Workflow Actually Costs $20k in DevOps 😭
item.description: I’ve been spending a lot of time lately looking at different AI automation setups. Mostly, I've just been trying to figure out where the actual leverage is for smaller engineering and ops teams....

item.title: API Versioning: Current Approaches and Choices in the Ecosystem
item.description: The moment you launch an API, one of the biggest nightmares associated with it is versioning decisions. Especially when you consider that this API will be used by different clients or is expected...

item.title: Building a Dating App with No Backend: How I used Rust, Tauri 2.0, and P2P Mesh Networking to Fight the Loneliness Pandemic
item.description: The Problem: The "Skinner Box" of Modern Dating Traditional dating apps have a fundamental conflict of interest: if you find a partner, they lose a customer. Their algorithms are designed to keep...

item.title: aion-indian-market-calendar: Python market calendar for NSE, BSE, MCX, and is-market-open checks in India.
item.description: If you build for Indian markets, market timing should not live as hardcoded if/else logic inside bots, cron jobs, or trading scripts. aion-indian-market-calendar is a Python package for Indian...

item.title: Meet Elias Thorne, top 50 oncology author: a case study in AI agents at scale
item.description: There is a small veterinary clinic in south Austin called Manchaca Road Animal Hospital. It has around 700 Facebook followers, a real address, a real phone number, a real team photo. Over the past...

item.title: I squeezed my iGPU dry, then added an eGPU — a GPU buying guide for AI on mini PCs
item.description: Last month, I hit a wall with my local LLM setup. Here's the full story — from software optimization to OCuLink eGPU to picking the right RTX 5060 Ti 16GB, with real pricing and brand teardown...

item.title: The haves and have nots of the AI gold rush
item.description: The vibes around the current AI boom aren't great, even in the tech industry.

item.title: Research repository ArXiv will ban authors for a year if they let AI do all the work
item.description: ArXiv is doing more to crack down on the careless use of large language models in scientific papers.

item.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
item.description: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok have settled the first lawsuit of its kind, alleging that social media addiction has cost public schools massive amounts of money, according to Bloomberg. The suit, filed...

item.title: Sony tries to explain that its AI Camera Assistant doesn’t suck
item.description: After Sony drew some unwanted attention for a post demonstrating its AI Camera Assistant on the Xperia 1 XIII, it's trying to clarify how the feature works. The company says it doesn't edit...

item.title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
item.description: OpenAI's latest shakeup comes as the company reportedly plans to combine ChatGPT and its programming product Codex.

item.title: Funnel Builder Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables WooCommerce Checkout Skimming
item.description: A critical security vulnerability impacting the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress has come under active exploitation in the wild to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout...

item.title: NPR’s Manoush Zomorodi talks about living with too much tech
item.description: Manoush Zamorodi is an accomplished reporter, podcast host, and author. Her new book, Body Electric, takes a comprehensive look at how technology is impacting our physical health. It's a...

item.title: Turtle Beach made a good SteelSeries headset clone that’s $50 less
item.description: I really liked the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless, so the company didn't have to say much to get me excited about its successor. The $399 Nova Pro Omni that came out on May 5th has a similar...

item.title: Here are 40 of our favorite deals from REI’s massive Anniversary Sale
item.description: REI’s annual Anniversary Sale — the retailer’s biggest of the year — has arrived, bringing with it discounts on all kinds of outdoor essentials. If you’ve got a camping trip coming up, now is a...

item.title: Mixtape is a musical portrait of teenage life
item.description: Playing Mixtape is like playing a video game version of a high school movie. Kids banter about the meaning of life and the theme songs that would play when they walk in a room. They're worried...

item.title: The app you need to clean up your computer
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 128, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, sorry this one's so Mac-heavy, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: These are the laptops I recommend for pretty much anyone
item.description: Need a new laptop? It’s a tough decision. If you’re like most people, a laptop is one of the most expensive tech purchases you’ll make, and it’s something you’ll use and live with nearly every day...

item.title: Some Asexuals Are Using AI Companions for Intimacy Without the Sex
item.description: “I’ve got one hand on the keyboard, one hand down below,” an artist who role-plays with their chatbot tells WIRED. But some asexual advocates aren’t thrilled about the association.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Bigfin Squid
item.description: Article about the bigfin squid. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: OpenAI and Malta partner to bring ChatGPT Plus to all citizens
item.description: OpenAI and Malta partner to expand AI access, offering ChatGPT Plus and training to help citizens build practical AI skills and use AI responsibly.

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 3: Elon Musk and Sam Altman traded blows over each other’s credibility. Now the jury will pick a side.
item.description: In the final week of the Musk v. Altman trial, lawyers traded blows over Elon Musk’s and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s credibility. Altman was grilled on his alleged history of lying and self-dealing...

item.title: Xbox is now XBOX
item.description: Xbox just allcapsmaxxed: Meet XBOX. This isn't a joke; Microsoft appears to be actually rebranding Xbox to XBOX. Asha Sharma, Xbox CEO, ran a poll on X earlier this week, asking fans whether...

item.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
item.description: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection program to all users over the age of 18 - meaning just about anyone can have the platform hunt for potential deepfakes of themselves. The likeness...

item.title: The OpenAI trial wraps up, and the Musk founder machine keeps spinning
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial came to a close this week, and the final arguments kept circling back to one question: can we trust the people in charge of AI? All of this is playing out as SpaceX...

item.title: Silicon Valley’s vacationland needs a new energy provider just as AI is driving prices up
item.description: Lake Tahoe, Silicon Valley's favorite vacation spot, is about to get hit with higher energy prices as AI drives demand for electricity.
